channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: As Palestinians mark Nakba, Israelis celebrate conquest of East Jerusalem
item.description: On May 15, Palestinians commemorated the Nakba ("catastrophe" in Arabic), referring to the forced exodus of Palestinians during the creation of the State of Israel in 1948. Around the same time, Israelis celebrated what they call the "reunification of Jerusalem" in 1967, meaning the occupation and then annexation of the Palestinian part of the city. One land, two historical narratives. Is it still possible to reconcile them? FRANCE 24's Claire Duhamel and Amira Souilem report.
item.dc:creator: Claire Duhamel

item.title: Beijing as centre stage: Trump & Putin mark 'series of diplomatic visits to the court of Xi Jinping'
item.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Dr. Flavia Lucenti, Postdoctoral Researcher at LUISS University for the EU Horizon Project REMIT. With Vladimir Putin in Beijing less than a week after Donald Trump’s own visit to China, Dr. Lucent emphasizes the historic significance in the sequencing of visits, revealing China’s increasingly central diplomatic role. She explores how the wars in Ukraine and Iran are reshaping the Sino Russian partnership. And Dr. Lucenti also highlights the structural asymmetry in the relationship: while China purchases a substantial share of Russian exports, “Russia accounts only 4% of China international trade”.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kentucky primary shows Republicans who criticise Trump 'signing death warrants'
item.description: Kentucky US Rep. Thomas Massie lost his Republican House primary Tuesday, becoming the latest Republican lawmaker to anger President Donald Trump and then fall to a primary challenger backed by the president. Although Trump's popularity may be falling among the American public, the result shows the president still has an iron grip on the Republican party, says France 24's Philip Turle.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump purges another Republican critic with Massie defeat in Kentucky
item.description: US President Donald Trump scored another victory in his campaign to punish disloyal Republicans on Tuesday ​as Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky lost his primary race, underscoring the risks for lawmakers who defy Trump. Massie, who angered Trump by leading a push to release Justice Department files tied to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and emerged as an outspoken critic of the war with Iran, was defeated by Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL backed by the president and bolstered by heavy spending by pro-Israel groups.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Los Angeles World Cup workers vow strike if ICE deployed at stadium
item.description: Workers at Los Angeles' SoFi Stadium vowed Monday to go on strike if federal immigration enforcement agents are deployed at the venue when it hosts World Cup matches next month. The UNITE HERE Local 11, representing around 2,000 hospitality employees, is demanding federal guarantees that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) will not be used in any of the eight World Cup matches scheduled to take place at the stadium.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Xi and Putin - Leaders praise 'unshakable foundations' at Beijing talks
item.description: Vladimir #Putin is visiting #Russia's most important partner since the start of the war in #Ukraine: #China. The two leaders are mainly set to discuss energy and security. The visit comes less than a week after #US President Donald #Trump's visit to #Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: WHO evaluates vaccines as efforts ramp up to control Ebola outbreak
item.description: The WHO said Tuesday it was examining whether any candidate vaccines or treatments could be used to rein in what it warned could be a lengthy Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The WHO has declared the surge of the highly contagious haemorrhagic fever, suspected to have killed 131 people and infected more than 500, an international health emergency.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran warns of war ‘beyond the region’ if US resumes attacks
item.description: Iran has warned the United States against resuming attacks, threatening to open new fronts if the current ceasefire ends following remarks from Donald Trump that the US may have to deliver another “big hit” against Tehran. Meanwhile, Iranian civilians are preparing for a new flare-up, as negotiations have yet to lead to any concrete decisions.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Energy in spotlight as Putin visits China
item.description: President Xi Jinping welcomed president Putin to Beijing on Wednesday for a bilateral summit set to last two days. In his opening remarks Xi stressed the need for the cessation of hostilities in the Middle East to stablise energy supplies and the pair discussed the "Power of Siberia 2" natural gas pipeline, although no deal on it was reached.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Arsenal clinch Premier League title after 22-year drought
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, May 20, 2026: The 17th Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo is getting a lot of coverage. Papers are looking into the impact of recent aid cuts on the emergency response plan. In the United States, President Donald Trump's battle with the Internal Revenue Service continues to make headlines. Also, The New York Times discusses Trump's World Cup delegate, Andrew Giuliani. Finally, the Gunners are back on top, as Arsenal win their 14th Premier League title.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: WHO says risk of global Ebola spread is low, but high at national, regional levels
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday said the Ebola epidemic risk was high at the national and regional levels and low at the global level.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Cannes pays tribute to ‘Thelma and Louise’ in festival poster
item.description: Every year, the Cannes Film Festival draws up a new poster. It always features an especially striking still from movie history, and it is then plastered up all over town and appears on pencils, fridge magnets and mugs. This year, they’ve picked a moment from Ridley Scott’s feminist fable, Thelma and Louise, which first premiered at the festival right back in 1991. Alison Sargent has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: San Diego mosque shooters radicalized online, says FBI
item.description: Two teenagers who shot and killed three people in an attack on a San Diego mosque were radicalized online where they first met and shared white supremacist views, according to authorities and writings they authored. The 17- and 18-year-old assailants took their own lives shortly after Monday's shooting.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Court names Mango tycoon's son as suspect in probe into father's death
item.description: The son of Isak Andic, founder of fashion group Mango, was named by a judge in Barcelona on Tuesday as a suspect in an investigation into his father's death in a 2024 cliff-top fall. Jonathan Andic, 45, was arrested ⁠and questioned by police in Catalonia, before later being released after posting 1 million euros in bail. France 24’s correspondent in Madrid Sarah Morris has the latest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: EU makes headway towards ratifying US trade deal, under pressure from Trump
item.description: After marathon five-hour talks, representatives from the EU Parliament and the EU Council agreed on Wednesday to go ahead and ratify the trade pact found with the US in July 2025. US President Donald Trump had threatened to increase tariffs on EU autos to 25 percent if the deal wasn't implemented by July 4. Also in this edition, talks between Samsung and its workers went down to the wire ahead of a scheduled 18-day strike on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: ‘US imperialism’: Anger in Philippines as war with Iran sends oil prices soaring
item.description: As Washington continues to struggle to reach an agreement with Iran that would permanently open the Strait of Hormuz, the impact of its closure is still being felt around the world, not least in the Philippines, where rising oil prices have pushed many to breaking point and anger, mostly directed to the US, has spilled into the streets. From Manila, our correspondent Jan Camenzind Broomby reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Xi hails China’s ‘unyielding’ ties with Russia as Putin visits Beijing
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing at the start of a visit focused on energy security. The meeting comes on the heels of US president Donald Trump’s visit to the Chinese capital. France 24’s Andy Hilliar takes a look at the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Crony capitalism and oligarchy': 'Remarkable correlation' between Adani's rise & Modi’s ascendancy
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Paranjoy Guha-Thakurta, Indian journalist, investigative author and filmmaker. He offers a sweeping critique of the nexus between oligarchic wealth, political power, and weakened institutional accountability in contemporary India. Drawing on the allegations surrounding billionaire industrialist Gautam Adani, U.S. sanctions investigations, and the politics of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, he argues that “crony capitalism and oligarchy” have become deeply embedded within the fabric of the Indian state.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Race to contain ebola outbreak in war-torn DRC where weak infrastructure hampers response
item.description: Annette Young welcomes Julie Drouet, DRC Director for Action Against Hunger. She offers a sobering assessment of the early Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Speaking from the frontline of a rapidly evolving health emergency, Drouet describes a humanitarian system racing against time in one of the world’s most fragile and conflict-affected regions. They are not only facing epidemiological challenges of containing Ebola, but also the deeper structural vulnerabilities that make outbreaks in eastern DRC uniquely perilous.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran ‘prefers diplomacy’ but if there’s a war, Tehran says it’s ready
item.description: Iran and the US traded threats as President Donald Trump warned of renewed strikes a day after he said he held off a major assault in hopes of a peace deal. Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says Iranian officials have maintained their position that “they prefer diplomacy. They don't see a military solution to this conflict,” he explains. “But if there's a war, they're ready for it.”
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Xi welcomes Putin in Beijing, hails China’s ‘unyielding’ ties with Russia
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing at the start of a visit focused on energy security. While Xi hailed his country's "unyielding" ties with Russia, Putin praised the "unprecedentedly high level" of bilateral cooperation despite "unfavourable external factors", without naming any third country.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League title for first time since 2004
item.description: Thanks to Manchester City's 1-1 draw at Bournemouth, Arsenal have won their 14th Premier League title.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: South Korean labour minister to mediate talks as Samsung faces strike threat
item.description: South ​Korea's ​labour minister will personally mediate talks ​between ‌Samsung ⁠Electronics and ‌its South Korean ⁠union, the ministry announced Thursday after the union threatened a strike following the collapse of talks on bonus payouts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
